===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. US President Donald Trump has reversed the 2009 "endangerment finding" regarding greenhouse gases. According to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, how much is this reversal expected to reduce spending for automobile manufacturers per vehicle?
(A) $2,400
(B) $1,400
(C) $2,500
(D) $1,000
(E) $2,100
explanation: The article explicitly states that White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters that reversing the finding would "reduce automobile manufacturers' spending by $2,400 per vehicle."
model_output:
Based on the White House press briefing transcript and multiple news reports attributing the statement to Karoline Leavitt, the reversal is expected to reduce spending for automobile manufacturers by $2,400 per vehicle.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0zdd7yl4vo)[[1]](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0zdd7yl4vo)

- [Roll Call Transcript] (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-press-briefing-karoline-leavitt-the-white-house-february-10-2026)[[3]](https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-press-briefing-karoline-leavitt-the-white-house-february-10-2026)

===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. Regarding the conclusion of Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, how many undocumented immigrants did Tom Homan state had been arrested during the operation?
(A) Roughly 800,000
(B) More than 4,000
(C) Approximately 1.6 million
(D) Exactly 5,000
(E) Just under 2,500
explanation: Tom Homan is quoted in the article stating that "More than 4,000 undocumented immigrants had been arrested" during the surge.
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from February 12, 2026, Tom Homan, serving as border czar in the Trump administration, announced the conclusion of Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota. He stated that more than 4,000 undocumented immigrants had been arrested during the operation[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2lr9w29zwyo)[[2]](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/trump-administration-homan-ice-operation-metro-surge-ending-rcna258720). This matches option (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2lr9w29zwyo)
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/trump-administration-homan-ice-operation-metro-surge-ending-rcna258720)

(B) $865,000
(C) $1.2 million
(D) $2.4 million

model_output:

Multiple sources from February 12, 2026, report varying amounts raised, but several credible reports (including from Us Weekly, TV Insider, and others) consistently cite $1.4 million as the figure reached in the initial 24 hours, aligning with Thursday morning updates.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. During the congressional hearing on the Jeffrey Epstein files, to what type of facility was Ghislaine Maxwell recently moved following a meeting with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche?
(A) A maximum security federal penitentiary
(B) A low-security Florida state prison
(C) A high-security facility in New York
(D) A minimum security all-women Texas prison camp
(E) A medium-security detention center in Arizona
explanation: The article specifies that Ghislaine Maxwell was moved from a "low-security" Florida prison to a "minimum security" all-women Texas prison camp.
model_output:
Based on recent news reports and congressional hearing details from early February 2026, Ghislaine Maxwell was moved to a minimum security all-women Texas prison camp (specifically, the Federal Prison Camp in Bryan, Texas) in August 2025, following a meeting with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche. This move was discussed and scrutinized during the hearing, with some lawmakers demanding she be sent back to a higher-security facility, but no further move has occurred as of February 12, 2026.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. A collision occurred near South America between two US Navy vessels during a replenishment-at-sea operation. Which specific ships were involved in the incident?
(A) USS Truxtun and USNS Supply
(B) USS Abraham Lincoln and USNS Supply
(C) USS Truxtun and USS Supply
(D) USS Arleigh Burke and USNS Caribbean
(E) USS Truxtun and USNS Kanawha
explanation: The article identifies the vessels as the "Arleigh Burke-class destroyer USS Truxtun and the Supply-class fast combat support ship USNS Supply."
model_output:
Based on the web search results, the collision occurred on February 12, 2026, in the Caribbean Sea (near South America) during a replenishment-at-sea operation. The ships involved were the USS Truxtun (an Arleigh Burke-class destroyer) and the USNS Supply (a Supply-class fast combat support ship).[[1]](https://www.wsj.com/us-news/two-u-s-navy-ships-collide-in-waters-near-south-america-e7480637?gaa_at=eafs&gaa_n=AWEtsqdX8TZ2gCRL5O3_7ctE2ErNPaQ5dvugE_IJkpeDmZu1PiiWXFbN2i36&gaa_sig=-gDc4sAIPmbculaE2SYRaBUSn4KEJTgPPW0e6LPpYDy-w6C3bzkPVJdtp3RAwp5ZwqZoeKRMRER8zZDya_kIMg%3D%3D&gaa_ts=698e3716)[[2]](https://www.jpost.com/international/article-886475)[[3]](https://www.indexbox.io/blog/us-navy-ships-collide-in-caribbean-during-resupply-maneuver) This matches option (A). Note that USNS Supply is correctly designated as a naval auxiliary ship (not USS Supply, as in option C).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. The airspace over El Paso, Texas, was recently closed, causing significant disruption. What was the specific radius of the flight restriction area published by the FAA?
(A) 5-mile radius
(B) 15-mile radius
(C) 20-mile radius
(D) 10-mile radius
(E) 25-mile radius
explanation: The article states: "The restriction covered a 10-mile radius around El Paso, including parts of southern New Mexico."
model_output:
The FAA issued a Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR) over El Paso, Texas, effective from late February 10, 2026, initially planned through February 20 or 21 (though it was lifted early). Based on the official NOTAM details (FDC 6/2233) and multiple reports, the restriction covered a 10-nautical-mile radius (centered near the El Paso VORTAC at coordinates approximately 31°48'26"N 106°22'35"W) from the surface up to 17,999 feet MSL, excluding Mexican airspace.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. The RCMP provided a timeline for the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge. At what specific local time did they receive the report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School?
(A) 13:47
(B) 14:00
(C) 13:15
(D) 13:45
(E) 13:30
explanation: The article states: "The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said they received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at 13:30 local time on Tuesday (20:30 GMT)."
model_output:
### Reasoning and Thought Process

The event occurred on February 10, 2026, with the RCMP providing timeline details in subsequent updates. Sources consistently point to the report being received around 1:20 p.m., but there is variation due to time zone differences (PST vs. local MST in Tumbler Ridge). Most sources use "approximately 1:20 p.m." (13:20 in 24-hour format), but one reliable source (BBC) specifies "around 13:30 local time" with a GMT conversion confirming local MST. This is the only time that directly matches an option, and it's consistent with approximations in other reports (e.g., students hearing gunfire around 1:30 p.m.). Other options like 13:15 or 13:45 are close but not explicitly stated in sources, while 14:00 would align with a PST-to-MST conversion of 13:00 but doesn't match the data.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
